text:
(4) Any person who intends to make an application in relation to a child under the 1980 Hague Convention in a Contracting State (as defined in rule 12.44) other than the United Kingdom shall, if the court is satisfied that that person intends to make such an application, be entitled to obtain a copy bearing the seal ( GL ) of the court of any order made in relation to the child under the 1989 Act or under the inherent jurisdiction, whether or not that person was a party to the proceedings in which the order was made.


[ F14 (5) For the purposes of this rule, “document” and “copy” have the meanings given in rule 21.1(3). ] ]


Textual Amendments


F10 Rule 29.12 substituted (6.4.2012) by The Family Procedure (Amendment) Rules 2012 (S.I. 2012/679) , rules 1 , 25 (with rule 30 )


F11 Words in rule 29.12(1) inserted (7.12.2015) by The Family Procedure (Amendment No. 3) Rules 2015 (S.I. 2015/1868) , rules 1(2) , 8(a)(i)


F12 Words in rule 29.12(1) inserted (7.12.2015) by The Family Procedure (Amendment No. 3) Rules 2015 (S.I. 2015/1868) , rules 1(2) , 8(a)(ii)


F13 Words in rule 29.12(3) inserted (7.12.2015) by The Family Procedure (Amendment No. 3) Rules 2015 (S.I. 2015/1868) , rules 1(2) , 8(b)


F14 Rule 29.12(5) inserted (7.12.2015) by The Family Procedure (Amendment No. 3) Rules 2015 (S.I. 2015/1868) , rules 1(2) , 8(c)


### Service of judgments and orders E+W


29.13. —(1) The court officer must, unless the court directs otherwise, serve a copy of a judgment or an order made in family proceedings to every party affected by it.
